author: @duncanw3861
text: how would one turn the filtered/washed niooh from powder into a solid electrode?

replies.author: @wwabete2009
replies.text:
Crush the niooh with some conductive material and mix with water and a binder ( PVA, CMC, SBR, PVDF or PTFE) and allow to dry slowly into a solid electrode 
You can make moulds according to your electrode design - round, flat square, rectangular……

author: @JeffreyTApczynski
text: Question: If I want to triple the amount of NiOOH, do I just multiple the ingredients by 3? Also, how do you calculate the amount of grams of NaClO there is in 150ml of bleach? These may be dumb questions but I'm more of a technology expert than I am a Chemistry person. Thanks.

replies.author: @justADeni
replies.text:
kind of late answering this.

Your first question, yes that will work.
Second question: he calculates the amount of bleach from concentration (bleach is usually 5 percent NaClO by weight)

replies.author: @schinderiapraemeturus6239
replies.text: Have you brought any nickel plate?  It's bloody expensive.   I bought a 100ft roll of nickel strip for about $35 USD, converted to nickel chloride this will be enough for well over a hundred grams.   I would like to use a NiO-HO/graphene matrix for the anode in a NiFe battery, with gelled KHO electrolyte.  more work but far less costly to build

author: @morningstarsci
text: You might want to also keep this cool during the addition of the NiCl2. This should be exothermic, so you would end up with decomposition of the hypochlorite anion.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate

replies.author: @morningstarsci
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Yeah, I was involved in making nickel (ii) peroxide a while back, and found it's not really well characterized. It seems to be a mixture of oxides, which is why the prep can be so variable.

author: @jsmythib
text: I tried this with nickel chloride in solution(its what I had) and having no idea the concentration I just added in the  bleach and sodium hydroxide solution. The nickel oxyhydroxide precipitated out and turned into a black goo. As i continued to add to the solution, it turned BACK INTO NICKEL CHLORIDE. So...as a novice- I stand confused!? :)

author: @tonymac96
text:
Robert...very good video....However, I'm still wondering...if you use only NiCl2 and NaOH...you will get a precipitate that is sparkling green...Is this precipitate Ni(OH)2 ???...There is this video (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vmflxUVr7BM&list=PL3aqEJPjK3gnEWcr16N8dx-Z5C7WuesOn&index=2&t=0s)  that says that this precipitate is NIO(OH)  (nickel Oxy-hydroxide)...but in your video, the precipitate is dark....what do you think?

Thanks

author: @Omn1cy
text: will either Nickel Chloride Hexahydrate or  Nickel chloride anhydrous work?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: sure just adjust for molarity

replies.author: @Omn1cy
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith Decided on Nickel Chloride Hexahydrate because its cheaper on ebay, not sure what to do about molarity. How would you calculate that?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
Mike Gimon
it's a hexahydrate so there are 6 molecules of water to every molecule of nickel chloride - so when you weigh out a mole of nickel chloride hexahydrate you have weighed out one mole of nickel chloride and 6 moles of water
